keyPoints: Constraint-based workflows defined: pre-specified prompts, mandatory checks, bounded iterations.
Within-subject randomized design to establish causal effects on over-trust, mode-collapse, and speed.
Outcome measures specified: speed metrics, decision quality, trust calibration, over-trust proxies, mode-diversity, and governance traceability.
Translate findings into actionable design heuristics and user protocols emphasizing auditability and human-in-the-loop governance.
Acknowledge evidence gaps and domain specificity; plan preregistration, replication, and cross-domain generalization considerations.
Benchmark expectations against Kang (2024) results, noting speed–quality trade-offs and practical interpretation for practitioners.
